items.content_text: A memorial endowment has been launched for a summer camp founded by women inspired by public health data. Why included: Mentions public health inspiration

items.title: Vermont regulators approve a trimmed increase to 2026 health insurance premiums for large employers - VTDigger
items.content_text: Vermont regulators have approved a 13.7% average increase to health insurance premiums for large employers charged by Blue Cross Blue Shield of Vermont. Why included: Names BCBSVT directly, legislative action on coverage
tags: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
matchedTerms: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
items.snippet: ...  double-digit increases to the premium rates that Vermont’s biggest health insurance provider can charge large employers next year. Vermont’s main health care regulator will allow Blue Cross Blue Shield of Vermont to increase the cost of health insurance premiums for Vermont employers with more than 100 employees by an average of 13.7% in 2026, according to a press release from the Green Mountain Care Board. The rate increase is expecte ...
items.summary: Vermont regulators have approved a 13.7% average increase to health insurance premiums for large employers charged by Blue Cross Blue Shield of Vermont.
items.reason: Names BCBSVT directly, legislative action on coverage

items.title: Sanders calls for investigation into vaccine committee firings
items.content_text: Senator Bernie Sanders is calling for an investigation into the firing of members of a vaccine committee. Why included: Vaccine policy and public health
items.snippet: Sanders calls for investigation into vaccine committee firings WASHINGTON – After Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. fired every member of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP) at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), Sen. Bernie Sanders (I ...
items.summary: Senator Bernie Sanders is calling for an investigation into the firing of members of a vaccine committee.
items.reason: Vaccine policy and public health

items.title: VTDigger Facebook post: BlueCross BlueShield wants to offer a new suite of cheaper plans, but the state’
items.content_text: BlueCross BlueShield wants to offer cheaper plans, but the state's healthcare regulator doubts they will yield real savings. Why included: Names BCBSVT directly, regulatory action on coverage Comments: Janet Fitzpatrick: Allow people over 30 to buy Blue Cross Blue Shield catastrophic plans! Those plans are much more affordable. | Mark Byland: sp/healthcare/healthprofit | Kristin Bryn Reid: The health insurance system in Vermont is voing exactly as designed for the last 20 or so years We tried to warn you | Dylan Greer: Or hear me out… we pay a boat load of money to insurance companies… they can pay for our healthcare | Janice Wright: Cheaper plan = lower monthly payments, but higher deductibles, higher co-pays, less coverage, and fewer in-network doctors | Jay Files: My work just switched to MVP gold great insurance we had blue cross it covers almost nothing worthless insurance you have to fight with them for everything | Amy Beth: "higher" deductible plan my deductible is already $9500 so unless they are significantly dropping the monthly premium which is between $809-900 a month for one person this isn't helpful. Health insurance for those of us who have to purchase it from the marketplace is ridiculous. | Ruth Miller: I dunno...I just got a class action suit settlement payment from BCBS. I'm done with them, especially after leaving me with no gap coverage after they pulled Blue Advantage out of VT. They seem to lose money on a continuing basis. | Beth Stevens: My plan for myself a child was supposed to be 650 a month with a 5k deductible, got my bill over 900 a month. I canceled as I was still paying for meds and co pay | Matthew Funk: It’s almost like y’all already Fcked it up
items.snippet: VTDigger's Post VTDigger 3h · BlueCross BlueShield wants to offer a new suite of cheaper plans, but the state’s primary healthcare regulator is casting doubt on whether that would actually yield any real savings.vtdigger.orgVermont’s largest health insurer wants to offer a cheaper plan. Can it actually help Vermonters? - VTDiggerAll reactions:815 comments2 sharesLikeCommentMost ...
items.summary: BlueCross BlueShield wants to offer cheaper plans, but the state's healthcare regulator doubts they will yield real savings.
items.reason: Names BCBSVT directly, regulatory action on coverage

comments.author: Janet Fitzpatrick
comments.text: Allow people over 30 to buy Blue Cross Blue Shield catastrophic plans! Those plans are much more affordable.

comments.author: Kristin Bryn Reid
comments.text: The health insurance system in Vermont is voing exactly as designed for the last 20 or so years We tried to warn you

comments.author: Dylan Greer
comments.text: Or hear me out… we pay a boat load of money to insurance companies… they can pay for our healthcare

comments.author: Janice Wright
comments.text: Cheaper plan = lower monthly payments, but higher deductibles, higher co-pays, less coverage, and fewer in-network doctors

comments.author: Jay Files
comments.text: My work just switched to MVP gold great insurance we had blue cross it covers almost nothing worthless insurance you have to fight with them for everything

comments.author: Amy Beth
comments.text: "higher" deductible plan my deductible is already $9500 so unless they are significantly dropping the monthly premium which is between $809-900 a month for one person this isn't helpful. Health insurance for those of us who have to purchase it from the marketplace is ridiculous.

comments.author: Ruth Miller
comments.text: I dunno...I just got a class action suit settlement payment from BCBS. I'm done with them, especially after leaving me with no gap coverage after they pulled Blue Advantage out of VT. They seem to lose money on a continuing basis.

comments.author: Beth Stevens
comments.text: My plan for myself a child was supposed to be 650 a month with a 5k deductible, got my bill over 900 a month. I canceled as I was still paying for meds and co pay

comments.author: Matthew Funk
comments.text: It’s almost like y’all already Fcked it up
